description: Update CLAN_POWERS tooltip texts in MainMenu.tsx with correct short summaries, and implement all clan power logic in gameLogic.ts
steps: 1. In src/components/MainMenu.tsx, replace the CLAN_POWERS record (lines 9-18) with correct short summaries for each clan: koi='Monedas como Ronin. Al inicio de Guerra cambia Ronin por Monedas. En Contratar Ronin, sus Monedas suman Fuerza.', sol='Al ganar empate por Honor, gana 1 Moneda + 1 PV. El perdedor pierde 1 Moneda + 1 PV.', loto='Elige cualquier Orden Politica, sin importar las fichas que robe.', tortuga='Fortalezas se mueven como figuras y cuentan como 1 de Fuerza.', libelula='Invoca figuras en cualquier Provincia. Mueve figuras a cualquier Provincia.', zorro='Al inicio de Guerra, coloca 1 Bushi gratis en cada provincia sin unidades propias.', bonsai='El coste maximo de cualquier compra es 1 Moneda.', luna='Todas sus figuras tienen Fuerza 2. Max 2 figuras por provincia y max 2 en Santuarios.'

2. In src/utils/gameLogic.ts initiateWarPhase function, BEFORE applyWarUpgrades, add Koi clan power: find the Koi player, swap all their ronin for the same number of coins (player.coins += player.ronin; player.ronin = 0). Log the swap.

3. In src/utils/gameLogic.ts initiateWarPhase function, AFTER Koi swap, add Zorro clan power: find the Zorro player, for each province where the Zorro player has zero figures, place 1 Bushi figure (from reserve: decrement player.bushi by 1 if available). Log placements.

4. In src/utils/gameLogic.ts resolveNextBattle function, where hire-ronin force is added (around line 'if (pid === hireRoninWinner) { force += player.ronin; }'), add Koi power: if the player's clanId is 'koi' AND they won the hire-ronin tactic, also add player.coins to force (player.coins count as ronin for Hire Ronin).

5. In src/utils/gameLogic.ts calculateForce function, add Luna power: if the figure's owner has clanId 'luna', set figForce to Math.max(2, figForce) instead of starting at 1. This makes all Luna figures have at least Force 2.

6. In src/utils/gameLogic.ts calculateForce function, add Tortuga power: after computing totalForce for a player, count fortress figures owned by that player in the province. If the player's clanId is 'tortuga', add 1 force per fortress in that province.

7. In src/utils/gameLogic.ts buySeasonCard function, add Bonsai power: after computing effectiveCost, if the player's clanId is 'bonsai', cap effectiveCost at Math.min(effectiveCost, 1). This means the Bonsai discount is applied BEFORE the issuer discount. Specifically: if card.cost >= 2, Bonsai pays 1. If issuer discount applies afterward, cost becomes 0.

8. In src/utils/gameLogic.ts buildFortress function, add Bonsai power: if the player's clanId is 'bonsai', the fortress costs 1 coin instead of 3. Change the cost check and coin deduction conditionally.

9. In src/utils/gameLogic.ts chooseMandateTile function, add Loto power: if the current player's clanId is 'loto', allow choosing ANY of the 5 mandate types (recruit, marshal, train, harvest, betray) regardless of what was drawn. Skip the 'chosenIdx === -1' validation for Loto. The chosen mandate is still removed from drawnMandates if present, but Loto can choose a mandate that was not drawn.

10. In src/utils/gameLogic.ts recruitPlaceFigure function, add Luna province limit: if the player's clanId is 'luna', check that there are fewer than 2 of that player's figures already in the target province. If there are already 2, reject the placement (return state).

11. In src/utils/gameLogic.ts, wherever temple/shrine placement logic exists (search for shinto placement in temples), add Luna temple limit: Luna can have at most 2 figures total across all 4 temples. If already at 2, reject placing more.

12. In src/utils/gameLogic.ts, for every honor tiebreak where the higher-honor player wins (harvest force tie at line ~815, war force tie, war tactic bid tie), add Sol power: if the winning player's clanId is 'sol', grant them +1 coin and +1 VP, and the losing player(s) get -1 coin and -1 VP (if they have coins/VP to lose). Log these Sol bonuses.
acceptance_criteria: TypeScript compiles without errors (npx tsc --noEmit passes)
CLAN_POWERS in MainMenu.tsx has correct concise Spanish descriptions for all 8 clans
Koi: ronin swapped for coins at war start in initiateWarPhase
Koi: coins add force when Koi wins hire-ronin tactic
Zorro: places free bushi in empty provinces at war start
Bonsai: max cost 1 for season cards and fortresses
Loto: can choose any mandate type regardless of drawn tiles
Luna: figures have base force 2, max 2 figures per province, max 2 in temples total
Tortuga: fortresses count as 1 force (already can move - that was pre-existing)
Libelula: already implemented (move/recruit anywhere) - verified preserved
Sol: gains 1 coin + 1 VP on honor tiebreak wins, opponent loses 1 coin + 1 VP
findings: All clan powers implemented and verified. TypeScript compiles cleanly. Luna province limit also added to moveForces (marshal movement). Temple shinto placement is handled in gameStore.ts (not gameLogic.ts) so Luna temple limit was added there. Sol power added to 4 tiebreak locations: harvest, war force, war tactic bids, and kami turn temples. UI fix: ActionPanel.tsx now shows all 5 mandate types when Loto is the current player, matching the game logic that already allows Loto to choose any mandate. Post-review fixes: (1) Fortress figures now skipped in calculateForce loop so non-Tortuga fortresses contribute 0 force and Tortuga fortresses correctly contribute 1 via post-loop addition. (2) Dead Luna monster code block removed. (3) Sol end-game VP tiebreak bonus added to resolveWinter. (4) Loto face-down mandate: added hidden field to Mandate interface, set in executeMandate when issuer is Loto, rendered as secret tile with kanji on PoliticsTrack.
